_meta.description: Peril-agnostic vulnerability classes. Each class carries the parameters every physical engine needs: a tropical-cyclone Emanuel `tc_v_half` (wind speed in m/s at 50% mean damage — lower = more vulnerable) and a flood depth-damage curve (mean damage ratio vs water depth in metres). An asset picks one class; each peril engine reads the matching parameters.
_meta.provenance: TC: Emanuel (2011) wind-damage form, ImpfTropCyclone.from_emanuel_usa(v_half=…); class v_half values are indicative (heavier structures less vulnerable). Flood: representative depth-damage ratios in the style of Huizinga et al. (2017, JRC global flood depth-damage functions). Earthquake: indicative MMI mean-damage-ratio curves differentiated by construction robustness (MMI 5–10). Indicative MVP values — see docs/METHODOLOGY.md.
